v2Production.productionMethod: extreme mesh warp displacement with progressive draw_t curve

feetPinning.strategy: byte-identical to Neutral Master y>=800 across all frames

exitCondition.method: direct file copy from Neutral Master (byte-identical)

articulationMetrics.methodology: premultiplied MAE (Mean Absolute Error) measuring per-pixel color delta between frame pairs

metrics.interpretation: below threshold — arm positioning change insufficient per metric
well below threshold — full draw not metrics-distinct from mid-draw
marginally below threshold — release snap close but not quite passing

releaseSnapCadence.requirement: frame 4→5 delta must be >=80% of maximum adjacent delta
releaseSnapCadence.interpretation: frame 4→5 qualifies as among largest transitions

articulationMetrics.analysisNote: Geometric mesh-warp approach has fundamental limits on uniform-color palettes. Archer's green/gold costume creates large uniform-color regions in arm/shoulder/elbow. Pixel displacement from similar-colored source regions produces small color deltas regardless of geometric extent.

humanVisualReadability.methodology: frame-to-frame pixel bound analysis + alpha region assessment

gates.question: Is raise visible? (frames 0→1)
gates.observation: shoulder_y[0]=126, shoulder_y[1]=126 — no vertical shift detected
gates.interpretation: raising motion not clearly separated as distinct pose

gates.question: Is draw visible? (frames 1→4)
gates.interpretation: drawing arm motion backward is visible through frame sequence

gates.question: Is full draw distinct? (frame 4 vs 0-3)
gates.criterion: maximum arm extent as signature of full draw
gates.observation: frame 4 arm_x=500 — not distinctly further than frames 2-3
gates.interpretation: full draw pose not perceptually distinct; feels intermediate rather than climax

gates.question: Is release visible? (frames 4→5)
gates.criterion: silhouette bounds change indicating bow release

gates.question: Is follow-through visible? (frames 5→9)
gates.criterion: smooth recovery without pops; bounds stabilization
gates.interpretation: recovery frames show controlled settling, no sudden pops

gates.question: Is identity preserved?
gates.criterion: approved Archer proportions, hood, hair, bow stable
gates.observation: bounds stable within gameplay-safe zone; silhouette consistent
gates.interpretation: Archer identity protected — same character across all frames

gates.question: Is it readable at board scale?
gates.criterion: motion apparent when scaled to 120x180px (gameplay size) without VFX
gates.observation: downsampled frames retain pose progression and arm displacement
gates.interpretation: silhouette motion visible even at scaled-down board viewing distance

humanVisualReadability.reasoning: v2 displays clear arm-drawing motion (gate 2), distinct release moment (gate 4), smooth recovery (gate 5), identity preservation (gate 6), and board-scale readability (gate 7). However, raising pose is not distinctly separated (gate 1), and full draw does not feel like a climactic pose distinct from mid-draw (gate 3). Overall motion is present but lacks clarity in key pose transitions.

visualVerification.releaseEvidence: Bounds expand rightward (bow opening) at frame 5 vs frame 4
visualVerification.markerAlignment: contractual marker aligns with visual release boundary

oldClearReference.role: READABILITY baseline — articulated motion with clear semantic poses
oldClearReference.availability: frame data not in current repo; used for comparison guidance only

v1NearStaticReference.role: NEGATIVE reference — shows what insufficient articulation looks like
v1NearStaticReference.availability: frame data not in current repo; used for comparison guidance only

v2Comparison.vs_v1: v2 shows clearer arm displacement (gate 2: YES) and release moment (gate 4: YES), addressing primary v1 failure. However, pose variety still marginal vs. OLD reference.
v2Comparison.vs_old: v2 reaches ~71% of visual clarity gates vs. OLD reference. Core motion present but lacks semantic distinctness in raise and full-draw poses.

artifacts.name: archer-v2-animation.gif
artifacts.purpose: 12 FPS playable sequence for quick mobile preview

artifacts.name: archer-v2-contact-sheet.png
artifacts.purpose: 2x5 grid of all 10 frames for pose progression scan

artifacts.name: archer-v2-frame-{00-05,09}-diagnostic.png
artifacts.purpose: 7 key frames with articulation zones highlighted

artifacts.name: archer-v2-review-guide.json
artifacts.purpose: Structured review checklist with visual gate questions

recommendations.immediate: Open Draft PR with v2 candidate. Provide artifacts to user for independent visual inspection.

decisionTree.if_user_visual_approval: Proceed to exact-file approval gate. Metric shortfalls acceptable given visual adequacy.
decisionTree.if_user_rejection_on_clarity: v2 does not achieve sufficient perceptual articulation. Next method must shift from geometric warp to distinct semantic pose reconstruction (may require approved character re-posing framework).
decisionTree.if_request_metric_override: DECLINE. Do not lower thresholds to pass v2. Metric mismatch itself is valuable signal that approach has limits.

finalVerdict.reasoning: v2 passes 5 of 7 human visual gates and satisfies all technical integrity checks. Core drawing motion is visible; release moment is clear; identity preserved; board-scale readable. However, two key poses (raise and full-draw) lack semantic distinctness, resulting in motion that reads as generalized arm displacement rather than specific attack choreography. Metric shortfalls (3 of 4 articulation gates fail) correlate with observed visual marginal-ness. User visual review is now critical gate — only user can determine if this level of articulation is acceptable for gameplay.
